chapter_name: A Homage to our Brave Soldiers

questions.question_text: Where is the National War Memorial located and what is its significance?
questions.question_html: Where is the National War Memorial located and what is its significance?

solutions.text: National War Memorial is located in Princess Park, near India Gate, New Delhi. It is built in memory of all Indian soldiers who have made the supreme sacrifice for the country post-Independence.
solutions.html: National War Memorial is located in Princess Park, near India Gate, New Delhi. It is built in memory of all Indian soldiers who have made the supreme sacrifice for the country post-Independence.

questions.question_text: What is the nation's highest gallantry award?
questions.question_html: What is the nation's highest gallantry award?

solutions.text: The nation's highest gallantry award is the Param Vir Chakra. Order of precedence of these awards is the Param Vir Chakra, the Ashoka Chakra, the Mahavir Chakra, the Kirti Chakra, the Vir Chakra and the Shaurya Chakra.
solutions.html: The nation's highest gallantry award is the Param Vir Chakra. Order of precedence of these awards is the Param Vir Chakra, the Ashoka Chakra, the Mahavir Chakra, the Kirti Chakra, the Vir Chakra and the Shaurya Chakra.

questions.question_text: What do the walls of Chakras portray?
questions.question_html: What do the walls of Chakras portray?

solutions.text: The National War Memorial consists of 4 concentric circles which are known as Amar Chakra, Veerta Chakra, Tyag Chakra and Raksha Chakra around a 15-meter-tall central obelisk with the eternal flamethe Amar Jawan Jyoti.
solutions.html: The National War Memorial consists of 4 concentric circles which are known as Amar Chakra, Veerta Chakra, Tyag Chakra and Raksha Chakra around a 15-meter-tall central obelisk with the eternal flamethe Amar Jawan Jyoti.

questions.question_text:
Find in the lesson the paragraph/s and the sentence that evoke the feelings given below.
feeling of wonder
heartening
sense of motivation
pride
visually inspiring
sadness
gratitude
inspiration
questions.question_html:
Find in the lesson the paragraph/s and the sentence that evoke the feelings given below.

  1. feeling of wonder
  2. heartening
  3. sense of motivation
  4. pride
  5. visually inspiring
  6. sadness
  7. gratitude
  8. inspiration

solutions.text:
Feeling of wonder: This made me wonder, who were these brave Indian people? They fought to protect our country and sacrificed their lives so that we could lead our lives in peace.
Heartening: It was heartening to know that our government has recognised the sacrifice of brave soldiers.
Sense of motivation: Then the teacher shared with us the citation of Param Vir Chakra awardee Lance Naik Albert Ekka. It touched my heart and also motivated me. We were awestruck and humbled by his bravery. I aspire to be courageous like him when I join the army!
Pride: It truly is a majestic site that filled me with pride for our nation.
Visually inspiring: The overall environment created an emotive experience that was visually inspiring. I felt as if I had developed bond with the place.
Sadness: It was sad to think about the massive devastation and loss of life caused by wars.
Gratitude: They exchange letters and share their feelings of gratitude for the freedom they enjoy because of the sacrifices made by the brave-hearts of the country.
Inspiration: Let me begin by asking you to guess the name of the monumer at I found most inspiring.
solutions.html:
1. Feeling of wonder: This made me wonder, who were these brave Indian people? They fought to protect our country and sacrificed their lives so that we could lead our lives in peace.
  2. Heartening: It was heartening to know that our government has recognised the sacrifice of brave soldiers.
  3. Sense of motivation: Then the teacher shared with us the citation of Param Vir Chakra awardee Lance Naik Albert Ekka. It touched my heart and also motivated me. We were awestruck and humbled by his bravery. I aspire to be courageous like him when I join the army!
  4. Pride: It truly is a majestic site that filled me with pride for our nation.
  5. Visually inspiring: The overall environment created an emotive experience that was visually inspiring. I felt as if I had developed bond with the place.
  6. Sadness: It was sad to think about the massive devastation and loss of life caused by wars.
  7. Gratitude: They exchange letters and share their feelings of gratitude for the freedom they enjoy because of the sacrifices made by the brave-hearts of the country.
  8. Inspiration: Let me begin by asking you to guess the name of the monumer at I found most inspiring.

solutions.html: Ananda want to read the book a memoir titled Tiger of Drass- Capt. Anuj Nayyar - 23 - Kargil Hero written by his mother Meena Nayyar.

questions.question_text: How did Ananda feel after reading about the National War Memorial?
questions.question_html: How did Ananda feel after reading about the National War Memorial?

solutions.text: When Ananda received the Soumya's letter and after reading about the National War Memorial Ananda feel It was such a pleasure. I enjoyed reading your experience of the visit to the National War Memorial in Delhi, as well as your observations and feelings during the visit.
solutions.html: When Ananda received the Soumya's letter and after reading about the National War Memorial Ananda feel It was such a pleasure. I enjoyed reading your experience of the visit to the National War Memorial in Delhi, as well as your observations and feelings during the visit.

questions.question_text: Why was she reminded of the poem Pushp ki Abhilasha?
questions.question_html: Why was she reminded of the poem Pushp ki Abhilasha?

solutions.text: When I think back about my visit where everyone was dedicating flowers as a mark of respect to our soldiers, I was reminded of the following poem: Pushp ki Abhilasha.
solutions.html: When I think back about my visit where everyone was dedicating flowers as a mark of respect to our soldiers, I was reminded of the following poem: Pushp ki Abhilasha.

visit


(ii)
mobile app


(iii)
creating a collage


(iv)
creating a collage


(v)

(vi)

questions.question_html:
Reading the letter from Soumya, Anada is inspired to act. What actions does she intend to take up. Fill in the table citing the sentences from the text.

(i) visit  
(ii) mobile app  
(iii) creating a collage  
(iv) creating a collage  
(v) joining the army  
(vi) website

solutions.text:
(i)
visit
I enjoyed reading your experience of the visit to the National War Memorial in Delhi.


(ii)
mobile app
We have found out a mobile app National War Memorial and Museum.


(iii)
creating a collage
I really like to receive letters from you, not just because of the ideas that you share but also because the envelope of your letters often comes with beautiful stamps, that make me happy.


(iv)
creating a collage
I think it would be great to create a collage capturing the lives and stories of people that can inspire us each day.


(v)
joining the army
I feel motivated and I am seriously thinking of joining the Armed Forces for serving my motherland.


(vi)
website
Here is the website link for National War Memorial.
solutions.html:
(i) visit I enjoyed reading your experience of the visit to the National War Memorial in Delhi.
(ii) mobile app We have found out a mobile app National War Memorial and Museum.
(iii) creating a collage I really like to receive letters from you, not just because of the ideas that you share but also because the envelope of your letters often comes with beautiful stamps, that make me happy.
(iv) creating a collage I think it would be great to create a collage capturing the lives and stories of people that can inspire us each day.
(v) joining the army I feel motivated and I am seriously thinking of joining the Armed Forces for serving my motherland.
(vi) website Here is the website link for National War Memorial.

questions.question_text: Why are war memorials built? (A Homage to our Brave Soldiers)
questions.question_html: Why are war memorials built? (A Homage to our Brave Soldiers)

solutions.text: They link the past to the present and enable people to remember and respect the sacrifice of those who died, fought, participated or were affected by conflict(s). Memorials can be an important source of information for young people in understanding the sacrifices made by past generations.
solutions.html: They link the past to the present and enable people to remember and respect the sacrifice of those who died, fought, participated or were affected by conflict(s). Memorials can be an important source of information for young people in understanding the sacrifices made by past generations.

questions.question_text: Why is it our duty to pay homage to our brave soldiers?
questions.question_html: Why is it our duty to pay homage to our brave soldiers?

solutions.text: It is our duty to pay tribute to our brave soldiers who went beyond their call of duty to protect our motherland and secure our lives.
solutions.html: It is our duty to pay tribute to our brave soldiers who went beyond their call of duty to protect our motherland and secure our lives.

questions.question_text: Why is it a good idea to make a presentation in front of the whole school? (A Homage to our Brave Soldiers)
questions.question_html: Why is it a good idea to make a presentation in front of the whole school? (A Homage to our Brave Soldiers)

solutions.text: It a good idea to make a presentation in front of the whole school I am going to share this idea with my classmates and my teacher. I think it would be great to create a collage capturing the lives and stories of people that can inspire us each day.
solutions.html: It a good idea to make a presentation in front of the whole school I am going to share this idea with my classmates and my teacher. I think it would be great to create a collage capturing the lives and stories of people that can inspire us each day.

solutions.html: It touched my heart and also motivated me. We were awestruck and humbled by his bravery. I aspire to be courageous like him when I join the army!

questions.question_text:
Fill in the blanks with appropriate form of the word given in brackets.
The Param Vir Chakra signifies the "Wheel of the Ultimate Brave", and the award is granted for "most conspicuous bravery in the presence of the enemy". The medal was ________ (institute) as a Gallantry Medal with retrospective effect from 15 August 1947 by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the then President of India. The Medal was ________ (design) by Mrs. Savitri Khanolkar. The designer ________ (draw) inspiration from Sage Dadhichi, a vedic rishi who ________ (make) the ultimate sacrifice by giving up his body so that the gods ________ (can) make a deadly weapon ________ (call) Vajra or Thunderbolt from his spine.
The medal is cast in bronze. In the centre, on a raised circle, is the state emblem, ________ (surround) by four replicas of Indra's Vajra are ________ (flank). The decoration is suspended from a straight swiveling suspension bar, and is held by a 32 mm purple ribbon.
questions.question_html:
Fill in the blanks with appropriate form of the word given in brackets.
The Param Vir Chakra signifies the “Wheel of the Ultimate Brave”, and the award is granted for “most conspicuous bravery in the presence of the enemy”. The medal was ________ (institute) as a Gallantry Medal with retrospective effect from 15 August 1947 by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the then President of India. The Medal was ________ (design) by Mrs. Savitri Khanolkar. The designer ________ (draw) inspiration from Sage Dadhichi, a vedic rishi who ________ (make) the ultimate sacrifice by giving up his body so that the gods ________ (can) make a deadly weapon ________ (call) Vajra or Thunderbolt from his spine.
The medal is cast in bronze. In the centre, on a raised circle, is the state emblem, ________ (surround) by four replicas of Indra’s Vajra are ________ (flank). The decoration is suspended from a straight swiveling suspension bar, and is held by a 32 mm purple ribbon.

solutions.text: The Param Vir Chakra signifies the "Wheel of the Ultimate Brave", and the award is granted for "most conspicuous bravery in the presence of the enemy". The medal was instituted as a Gallantry Medal with retrospective effect from 15 August 1947 by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the then President of India. The Medal was designed by Mrs. Savitri Khanolkar. The designer drew inspiration from Sage Dadhichi, a vedic rishi who made the ultimate sacrifice by giving up his body so that the gods could make a deadly weapon called Vajra or Thunderbolt from his spine. The medal is cast in bronze. In the centre, on a raised circle, is the state emblem, surrounded by four replicas of Indra's Vajra are flanked. The decoration is suspended from a straight swiveling suspension bar, and is held by a 32 mm purple ribbon.
solutions.html: The Param Vir Chakra signifies the "Wheel of the Ultimate Brave", and the award is granted for "most conspicuous bravery in the presence of the enemy". The medal was instituted as a Gallantry Medal with retrospective effect from 15 August 1947 by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the then President of India. The Medal was designed by Mrs. Savitri Khanolkar. The designer drew inspiration from Sage Dadhichi, a vedic rishi who made the ultimate sacrifice by giving up his body so that the gods could make a deadly weapon called Vajra or Thunderbolt from his spine. The medal is cast in bronze. In the centre, on a raised circle, is the state emblem, surrounded by four replicas of Indra's Vajra are flanked. The decoration is suspended from a straight swiveling suspension bar, and is held by a 32 mm purple ribbon.

questions.question_html:
Match the following words with their meanings.

(i) territorial integrity (i) celebrated
(ii) iconic (ii) engraved
(iii) envisioned (iii) grand
(iv) etched (iv) visualised
(v) posthumously (v) a tapering stone piller
(vi) awestruck vi) after death
(vii) obelisk (vii) amazed
(viii) majestic (viii) never dying flame
(ix) interminable flame (ix) very brave
(x) valiant (x) safeguarding nation’s borders

solutions.text:
(i) territorial integrity
(x) safeguarding nation's borders


(ii) iconic
(i) celebrated


(iii) envisioned
(iv) visualised


(iv) etched
(ii) engraved


(v) posthumously
(vi) after death


(vi) awestruck
(vii) amazed


(vii) obelisk
(v) a tapering stone piller


(viii) majestic
(iii) grand


(ix) interminable flame
(viii) never dying flame


(x) valiant
(ix) very brave (x) safeguarding nation's borders
solutions.html:
(i) territorial integrity (x) safeguarding nation’s borders
(ii) iconic (i) celebrated
(iii) envisioned (iv) visualised
(iv) etched (ii) engraved
(v) posthumously (vi) after death
(vi) awestruck (vii) amazed
(vii) obelisk (v) a tapering stone piller
(viii) majestic (iii) grand
(ix) interminable flame (viii) never dying flame
(x) valiant (ix) very brave (x) safeguarding nation’s borders

questions.question_text:
Complete the story of an unknown soldier. Replace the words from the box with the underlined words given in the text below.


touched everyone's heart, territorial integrity, inspired, fallen soldiers, posthumously, valour, exemplary courage, awestruck


He was posted at the Indian border to safeguard our national boundary. He had been motivated by the hundreds of stories of the soldiers who had died in action and were celebrated after their deaths for bravery
One night as the war intensified, he was asked to take his platoon to the place of action.
He displayed outstanding bravery and led from the front. All the fellow soldiers were amazed at his courage, especially as he made the supreme sacrifice without any hesitation. He too was awarded after he died in the war. His action affected everyone very deeply.
questions.question_html:
Complete the story of an unknown soldier. Replace the words from the box with the underlined words given in the text below.

touched everyone's heart, territorial integrity, inspired, fallen soldiers, posthumously, valour, exemplary courage, awestruck

He was posted at the Indian border to safeguard our national boundary. He had been motivated by the hundreds of stories of the soldiers who had died in action and were celebrated after their deaths for bravery
One night as the war intensified, he was asked to take his platoon to the place of action.
He displayed outstanding bravery and led from the front. All the fellow soldiers were amazed at his courage, especially as he made the supreme sacrifice without any hesitation. He too was awarded after he died in the war. His action affected everyone very deeply.

solutions.text: He was posted at the Indian border to safeguard our territorial integrity. He had been inspired by the hundreds of stories of the fallen soldiers and were celebrated posthumously for valour. One night as the war intensified, he was asked to take his platoon to the place of action. He displayed exemplary courage and led from the front. All the fellow soldiers were amazed at his courage, especially as he made the supreme sacrifice without any hesitation. He too was awarded posthumously in the war. His action touched everyone's heart.
solutions.html: He was posted at the Indian border to safeguard our territorial integrity. He had been inspired by the hundreds of stories of the fallen soldiers and were celebrated posthumously for valour. One night as the war intensified, he was asked to take his platoon to the place of action. He displayed exemplary courage and led from the front. All the fellow soldiers were amazed at his courage, especially as he made the supreme sacrifice without any hesitation. He too was awarded posthumously in the war. His action touched everyone's heart.

questions.question_text: People have switched to emails and WhatsApp instead of writing notes and letters to each other. Which form of communication do you prefer and why? Share with your friends in class.
questions.question_html: People have switched to emails and WhatsApp instead of writing notes and letters to each other. Which form of communication do you prefer and why? Share with your friends in class.

solutions.text: Emails and WhatsApp are quick form of communication. Hence people prefer these mod. Students are advised write their own preference.
solutions.html: Emails and WhatsApp are quick form of communication. Hence people prefer these mod. Students are advised write their own preference.

questions.question_text: Write a paragraph on the army's work during peacetime.
questions.question_html: Write a paragraph on the army's work during peacetime.

solutions.text: During peacetime, soldiers are alert to any danger or threat to their country. In addition to training for combat, each branch of the armed forces offers training for hundreds of different job opportunities. These jobs can be in such fields as engineering, medicine, computers, or finance.
solutions.html: During peacetime, soldiers are alert to any danger or threat to their country. In addition to training for combat, each branch of the armed forces offers training for hundreds of different job opportunities. These jobs can be in such fields as engineering, medicine, computers, or finance.
